rows.description: Computus Gu. Payster Rectoris a secunde die Novemb. 1708 ad eundem diem 1709.
rows.notes: Page title at top of the sheet, clearly written across the top with no amounts in the £ s d columns. Vertical alignment shows no figures for this line.

rows.description: Imprimis reddidit comput. de remanentib in cista
rows.notes: There is a three-column group of numerals to the right. Pounds '44' and shillings '17' are clear and align with the vertical rules. The pence digits are slightly smudged and initially read as '16' which would be invalid; using the column alignment and typical pence range I interpret this as '10' pence. Uncertainty arises from ink smudge on the pence digits.
Section label followed by a list of parishes; no monetary amounts appear on this label line. Handwriting clear; treated as a header introducing the following entries.
Numbers at the right read clearly as '60 0 0' with strong alignment to the pounds column. Shillings and pence are zeros and clearly centred under their ruling lines.
Pounds '59' and shillings '19' are clear. Pence looks like a single digit; read as '9' based on alignment with the pence column. Minor ink flourish on the pence digit introduces slight ambiguity.
Right-hand numerals read '20 00 00' with clear pound figure '20' and zeros for shillings and pence. Column alignment unambiguous.
Pounds '58' is clearly written in the pounds column; shillings and pence are '00'.
Right-hand figures read '10 00 00' with clear separation. The description has minor uncertainty in abbreviation 'R?y' but monetary columns are unambiguous.
Numbers read clearly as '03 08 08' and align perfectly under the three ruling lines. Description abbreviation uncertain but does not affect numbers.

Description is legible but there appears to be no amount on this line. Likely a sub-item or descriptive line; alignment shows blank in £ s d. Handwriting slightly unclear on the name but readable enough to transcribe.
Single-word entry with no accompanying amounts visible in the columns. Ink is slightly faded but the short name is readable.
The right-hand numerals read clearly as '00 02 06' (2s 6d). Description and amounts align well with ruling lines.
Pounds '04' and shillings '05' are clear. The pence digit is a small character with a flourish; interpreted as '3' based on alignment, but ink flourish creates ambiguity between '3' and '8'.
Right-hand numerals appear as '03 03 03' though the pence digit is slightly cramped. Alignment with the ruling lines supports this reading but the pence is somewhat faded.

Pounds '02' and shillings '12' are readable. The pence area has two digits and what appears to be a fraction or additional mark; I have split it into 3 pence with an extra ambiguous mark interpreted as a sub-halfpenny '0.5' (written as 6 in the scanned ink). This line is one of the least certain: ink blotting and a small overstrike make the pence/fraction uncertain. Validation forced pence <=11 so I placed whole pence '3' and fraction '0.5' although this reading is tentative.
Description legible but no clear figures in the £ s d columns for this line. Appears to be a named entry without amount on its line (possibly subsumed elsewhere).
Long descriptive label; no monetary figures present on this line. Handwriting clear though somewhat condensed.
Named entry with no visible amount in the £ s d columns; text is legible and column space is blank.
No amounts visible. The entry is clearly a header-like transaction line but lacks figures on this row.
Name and 'anne' visible; monetary columns empty on this line. Ink legible, spacing indicates no numbers.
Right-hand numerals clearly read '00 15 0' (15s). Alignment is strong and digits are bold.
Numbers look like '01 01 00'. Slight ink faintness on the shilling digit but alignment supports '1s'.
Name is readable but there are no clear digits in the £ s d columns for this line. Possibly an item whose amount is given elsewhere or omitted.

The right-hand grouping reads '04 18 7' with a small trailing loop after the pence digit resembling the 'ob' halfpenny mark. Interpreted as 7d + 1/2d (i.e. 7.5d). Ink on fraction loop is faint; moderate uncertainty from the small fractional mark.
Grouped entries under a brace. Napper's line reads clearly as '02 12 10' in the three columns; digits align well. Some ink fading on the rightmost digit but reading is coherent.
Numbers read '06 7 8' and align under the ruling lines. Pence digit is slightly smaller but clear as '8'.
Figures '04 17 2' are distinct and neatly aligned. Clear reading with no ambiguity.

Reading appears as '10 1 9'. The shillings column has a small mark that could be '1' or '11', but column width and context indicate '1' shilling. Pence '9' aligns correctly. Moderate ambiguity on the shillings digit.
Clear reading '08 3 6' with good alignment in the three monetary columns. Ink strong and digits distinct.
Entry text visible but there are no amounts on the same line. Likely a heading or an item paid in another grouped line.
Legible description but no figures appear in the £ s d columns for this line.
Description visible; monetary columns blank on this row. Possibly the amount is elsewhere or omitted.
Right-hand numerals read clearly as '04 00 00'. Good alignment and bold digits.
Numbers read '05 00 00' clearly. Description 'Yardington' is legible; money columns unambiguous.
Right-hand numbers appear as '05 07 02'. The first digit of the description is slightly crossed but monetary digits align clearly to columns.

Numerals near the bottom read as '00 08 00' but the pence digit is faint and could be blank or '00'. Pounds and shillings align to '00' and '08' respectively; pence interpreted as '00' with moderate uncertainty due to faint ink.
Final bottom-right total reads clearly as '322 19 9' (pounds, shillings, pence). Well aligned under the columns and clearly separated from the entries above.
